candidates.exposure_rationale: Pure-play housing finance; ~100% of revenue is the mortgage spread; market borrowings (NCD/CP/bank lines) fund the large majority of the book, so the 50 bps roll-down hits nearly the whole funding stack. [FY25 Annual Report (Ind AS), borrowings note - fixture cite]
candidates.liquidity_note: Mid-cap, adequately liquid for institutional sizing.
candidates.caveats: Mortgage pricing competition can pass the benefit to borrowers faster than modelled (SF-002 sensitivity).

candidates.exposure_rationale: Large pure-play HFC; heavy NCD funding mix rolls onto lower yields over 1-2 quarters; scale dampens the per-unit effect vs CND-001. [FY25 Annual Report (Ind AS), borrowings note - fixture cite]
candidates.caveats: Parentage/governance questions belong to the research swarm's management-governance module.

candidates.exposure_rationale: Vehicle/consumer NBFC with market-borrowing funding; shorter asset duration passes some benefit to borrowers, diluting capture. [FY25 Annual Report (Ind AS) - fixture cite]
candidates.caveats: Secondary-tier expression; competitive pass-through is the main dilution.

candidates.exposure_rationale: High EBLR-linked floating retail share with competitive deposit costs - the NIM-compression expression of HARM-001 for the pair construction. [FY25 Annual Report, EBLR disclosure - fixture cite]
candidates.liquidity_note: Liquid; futures available for the short leg.
candidates.caveats: Bank-specific deposit franchise strength can mute the compression; the pair isolates transmission asymmetry, not bank quality.
